container.intro: Complete 150 hours of state-approved training, submit a Live Scan background check, and pass the competency evaluation in California.

hero.title: Becoming a CNA in California
hero.dek: Learn CDPH requirements, find a 150-hour approved training program, and prepare for the state evaluation.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in California | CDPH Requirements
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to getting your CNA license in California. Learn about the 150-hour training requirement, Live Scan background checks, and the CDPH exam.
target_queries: how to become a cna in california

becoming.faq_heading: Common Questions about Getting a CNA in California

sections.label: Basic Requirements
sections.body: To train as a CNA in California, you must be 16 years old, complete Live Scan fingerprinting, and clear a background check.

faq.q: How many hours of training do I need to be a CNA in California?
faq.a: California requires more training than the federal minimum: a total of 150 hours, consisting of 50 hours of theory and 100 hours of clinical training.

hero.title: California CNA State Exam Application
hero.dek: Apply via CDPH form 283B to take the Nurse Assistant competency evaluation.

seo.title: California CNA Exam Application (CDPH 283B) | Registry Guide
seo.description: How to apply for the California CNA state exam using form CDPH 283B and get your official Nurse Assistant certification.

sections.label: Application Steps
sections.body: Submit the Initial Application (CDPH 283B) before or during your training program, complete Live Scan, and pass both parts of the competency test.

faq.q: What form is required to apply for CNA testing in California?
faq.a: You must submit the CDPH 283B Certified Nurse Assistant Initial Application to the Aide and Technician Certification Section.

hero.title: Transfer a CNA License to California
hero.dek: California allows active out-of-state CNAs to transfer via Endorsement (Reciprocity) without retaking the training.

seo.title: Transfer CNA License to California | Endorsement Steps
seo.description: Learn how to transfer your active out-of-state CNA license to CDPH in California using forms 283B and 931 for Nurse Assistant endorsement.

transfer.faq_heading: Questions about Transferring your CNA to California

sections.label: Transfer Requirements
sections.body: Submit forms CDPH 283B and CDPH 931. You must show an active out-of-state certification and clear a California Live Scan background check. If certified out-of-state for more than 2 years, proof of recent paid work is required.

faq.q: How do I transfer my CNA license to California?
faq.a: Submit the CDPH 283B Initial Application and CDPH 931 Verification form, pass a Live Scan background check, and provide proof of recent work if your license is over two years old.
